reason: Adjusting the ucredit parameter in /etc/security/pwquality.conf enforces a password complexity requirement for uppercase characters. This is a standard PAM password policy change that does not affect active sessions, authentication mechanisms, or system services, and only impacts password changes at the next rotation.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Existing passwords lacking an uppercase character will be rejected at the next password change, potentially causing user inconvenience; Misconfiguration could inadvertently disable the requirement if the value is set incorrectly
